headlines.title: Chris Mason: Mandelson nightmare haunts Starmer again
headlines.description: The prime minister is believed to be absolutely furious over the handling of Lord Mandelson's vetting, Chris Mason writes.

headlines.title: Finance ministers and top bankers raise serious concerns about Mythos AI model
headlines.description: Experts say Mythos potentially has an unprecedented ability to identify and exploit cyber-security weaknesses.

headlines.title: Lamborghini among 160,000 cars seized as uninsured driving reaches 17-year high
headlines.description: Seizure numbers hit a 17-year high as an estimated 300,000 uninsured vehicles are driven each day.

headlines.title: Harry and Meghan meet Bondi shooting survivors
headlines.description: On the fourth day of their Australian visit, the royal couple pay tribute to the 15 people killed in the Bondi shooting.

headlines.title: Singer D4vd arrested on suspicion of murdering teenage girl
headlines.description: The remains of 14-year-old Celeste Rivas Hernandez were found in the singer's car last year.

headlines.title: UK seeks closer EU ties in volatile times - but at what cost?
headlines.description: The UK is adopting a "ruthlessly pragmatic" approach to becoming closer to its European neighbours, the UK's EU minister tells the BBC.

headlines.title: Three charged with arson on Persian media offices
headlines.description: Two teenagers and a 21-year-old man are due in court charged with arson with intent to endanger life.

headlines.title: Rising value of Pokémon cards sparks smash and grab crime spree
headlines.description: Small shops across the UK are being targeted by thieves stealing collectibles worth thousands of pounds.

headlines.title: Judge halts above-ground construction of Trump's White House ballroom
headlines.description: The judge allows the underground bunker portion of the project to proceed, while the US president says the ballroom "is needed now".

headlines.title: Artemis commander tells BBC about 'powerful' moment crater named after his late wife
headlines.description: Reid Wiseman’s two daughters were in Nasa’s mission control room for the naming of the “Carroll” crater in honour of the commander’s late wife.

headlines.title: What we know about the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire
headlines.description: A 10-day truce between the two countries is now in effect, with Iran-backed Hezbollah voicing support, as negotiations continue between the US and Iran.

headlines.title: Trump's Lebanon ceasefire takes Israel by surprise
headlines.description: Few Israelis see this truce as a way out of the conflict with Hezbollah, the BBC's Lucy Williamson writes.

headlines.title: Lyse Doucet in Iran: Destruction shows huge civilian cost of the war
headlines.description: While military targets have been struck in Iran, civilian areas have too, showing the stark reality of the war.

headlines.title: No issues with UK fuel supply, says Reeves
headlines.description: The chancellor was speaking at the end of the International Monetary Fund meeting in Washington.

headlines.title: Avengers reassemble and Top Gun flies back - Hollywood studios preview their new movies
headlines.description: Some of the most hotly anticipated new films of the next couple of years are previewed at CinemaCon.

headlines.title: Christine Baranski says West End debut is a 'dream come true'
headlines.description: The US actress will star opposite Richard E Grant in a new production of Noel Coward's comedy Hay Fever.

headlines.title: Weekly quiz: What did Trump say about the Pope?
headlines.description: How much attention did you pay to what happened in the world over the past seven days?

headlines.title: The Papers: 'Starmer in peril' and 'Gunners hero dead'
headlines.description: Calls for PM to resign over Mandelson vetting and ex-Arsenal goalie's 'train horror' death leads the papers.

headlines.title: Family 'skimped to get by' during toddler's cancer treatment
headlines.description: There is a bespoke fund to cover travel costs for families of children undergoing cancer treatment - but it only applies in England.

headlines.title: Is the UK finally waking up to the power of video games?
headlines.description: The UK's biggest video games awards cap off a week of big announcements, but will they change anything?

headlines.title: 'I was tortured and lost my hand' - one student's struggle to get an education in Nigeria
headlines.description: The BBC speaks to a student who pushed for his his toe print to be taken to verify his identity.

headlines.title: South Korea's runaway wolf finally captured after nine-day search
headlines.description: The search has been marked by twists and turns, gripping South Korea and even inspiring a meme coin.

headlines.title: Afghan veteran hospitalised after station assault
headlines.description: Aziz Ahmadzai had been working as a security guard at Weymouth Railway Station when he collapsed.

headlines.title: Champions League in the Championship? Forest's juggling act goes on
headlines.description: Nottingham Forest continue their remarkable Europa League run - but does it only increase the pressure on staying in the Premier League?

headlines.title: Australia's most decorated living soldier released on bail over war crime charges
headlines.description: Lawyers argued that Ben Roberts-Smith wouldn't be able to defend himself properly from prison.

headlines.title: Peacock terrorises 92-year-old farmer's chickens
headlines.description: The peacock initially got along with the chickens before running riot on the farm, the farmer says.

headlines.title: Dog walker beaten with hockey stick by top horse trainer says his jail term is too soft
headlines.description: Martin Dandridge, 72, had his arm broken in the attack, leaving him with “ongoing pain” in his forearm.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Mandelson Failed Security Vetting…Who Knew What, When?
headlines.description: Starmer did not know Mandelson failed vetting, government says

headlines.title: Has Trump given up on the midterms?
headlines.description: The president is losing public support over the Iran war.

headlines.title: Imagine a world where no one has heard of The Beatles
headlines.description: An unsuccessful singer-songwriter wakes up in a world where no-one remembers The Beatles.

headlines.title: Is this the beginning of the end for LIV Golf?
headlines.description: With speculation continuing over LIV Golf's future, BBC Sport analyses whether the breakaway tour will continue.

headlines.title: Italy made me a manager when England 'discouraged' me - Cole
headlines.description: Ashley Cole won 107 caps for England, seventh on the all‑time list, but says he was "discouraged" by those in the football pyramid from becoming a head coach.

headlines.title: 'I'd run the M4 naked' - Williams targets snooker history
headlines.description: Mark Williams could cause traffic chaos on the motorway if he surpasses Ronnie O'Sullivan as the oldest ever world champion.

headlines.title: Nobody says who called first, but Arteta and Guardiola speak again - Balague
headlines.description: As Manchester City prepare to host Arsenal in Sunday's crucial Premier League meeting between the top two, European football expert Guillem Balague looks at how the two managers have evolved.

headlines.title: Eight famous all-English European ties as Villa & Forest set up semi-final
headlines.description: Here are eight of the most memorable all-English clashes in Europe as Aston Villa and Nottingham Forest set up a Europa League semi-final.

headlines.title: Sutton's predictions v boxer Molly McCann & the Boo Radleys
headlines.description: Chris Sutton takes on boxer Molly McCann and The Boo Radley's frontman Sice Rowbottom plus the BBC readers and AI with his predictions for this weekend's Premier League fixtures.
